steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the most logical completion of the text. The key facts: the two censuses reported similar counts of vertebrates, plants, and algae, so the large difference in total species must come from microorganisms. The text explains that researchers make "consequential" decisions about classifying microorganisms because their morphological variability is poorly understood. The blank must name the specific classification decision that drove the discrepancy.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Coll and colleagues reported a much higher number of species than Bianchi and Morri did largely due to the inclusion of invertebrate species that had not been described at the time of Bianchi and Morri's census.": Contradicts the text, which states that new invertebrate species explain only "part" of the difference, not the bulk of it. ✗
  • "some differences observed in microorganisms may have been treated as variations within species by Bianchi and Morri but treated as indicative of distinct species by Coll and colleagues.": Directly names the classification decision the passage is building toward — the same organisms were split differently by each team, explaining the gap without invoking new species. ✓
  • "Bianchi and Morri may have been less sensitive to the degree of morphological variation displayed within a typical species of microorganism than Coll and colleagues were.": Reverses the direction: lower sensitivity to within-species variation would lead to reporting more species, not fewer. Bianchi and Morri reported the lower count, so they must have been more sensitive (lumping more together), not less. ✗
  • "the absence of clarity regarding how to differentiate among species of microorganisms may have resulted in Coll and colleagues underestimating the number of microorganism species.": Coll and colleagues reported nearly double the species, so they overestimated relative to Bianchi and Morri, not underestimated. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
